name: pydeseq2
tagline: Guides bulk RNA-seq differential expression analysis with PyDESeq2: design, testing, shrinkage, export, and troubleshooting.
verdict: Clean, on-topic bioinformatics workflow skill with no security red flags: no credential access, no network exfiltration, no remote code piped into shell, pinned installs from PyPI, and explicit pickle-safety warnings. Minor nits: no negative-trigger guidance (single-cell vs bulk) and file runs to 577 lines, over the ~500-line budget, so some content could mo

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do not use for' guidance — e.g. does not clarify this is for bulk RNA-seq specifically, not single-cell, which a user could otherwise misapply the skill to.
File is 577 lines, exceeding the ~500-line budget; several code-heavy sections (visualization guidelines, common analysis patterns) could be trimmed toward the reference docs.
